title: The Metropolitan Museum of Art—The Met Fifth Avenue

admission.pricing_note: Adults $30; eligible local residents and regional students may pay what they wish; same-day entry covers both Met locations.

collection.summary: More than 1.5 million objects spanning 5,000 years and cultures worldwide.

building.scope_note: Approximate present-day area of the Fifth Avenue complex.
building.history: The Vaux–Mould building opened in 1880; Hunt's Fifth Avenue facade and Great Hall followed in 1902, with later wings surrounding the original structure.

financial_value.basis: Institution-wide audited net assets; the art collection is excluded from the balance sheet.

location.address: Constitution Avenue NW between 3rd and 9th Streets

admission.pricing_note: Admission is always free; no passes or tickets are required.

collection.summary: More than 160,000 works tracing Western art from the Middle Ages to the present.

building.scope_note: Year refers to the West Building; the campus also includes I. M. Pei's 1978 East Building and a sculpture garden.
building.history: Congress established the Gallery in 1937; the neoclassical West Building opened in 1941 and the modernist East Building in 1978.

financial_value.basis: Donor-restricted and board-designated private funds; federal appropriations fund core operations.

admission.pricing_note: Adult admission is $30 and includes galleries and special exhibitions.

collection.summary: Almost 200,000 works of modern and contemporary art, architecture, design, photography, film, and media.

building.scope_note: Area applies to the post-2019 53rd Street complex.
building.history: The original International Style building opened in 1939; major expansions by Taniguchi and Diller Scofidio + Renfro with Gensler opened in 2004 and 2019.

financial_value.basis: Audited endowment net assets at June 30, 2025.

admission.pricing_note: Standard adult admission is $32; lower rates apply to Chicago and Illinois residents.

collection.summary: Nearly 300,000 works spanning global art from antiquity through contemporary practice.

building.scope_note: Approximate area of the full multi-wing complex after the 2009 Modern Wing.
building.history: The Michigan Avenue building was constructed for the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition; Renzo Piano's Modern Wing opened in 2009.

financial_value.basis: Institution-wide endowment net assets for the combined museum and school organization.

title: J. Paul Getty Museum—Getty Center
institution_type: European art and photography museum campus

admission.pricing_note: Admission is free with a timed-entry reservation; parking is charged separately.

collection.summary: European paintings, drawings, manuscripts, photographs, sculpture, and decorative arts, chiefly from the medieval period through the early 20th century.

building.scope_note: Area covers the entire 12-building Getty Center complex, not only museum galleries.
building.history: The hilltop campus opened in 1997 after more than a decade of planning and construction and houses several Getty Trust programs.

financial_value.basis: Getty Trust-wide calendar-year endowment; not allocated to the Getty Center campus.

title: The Huntington Library, Art Museum, and Botanical Gardens
institution_type: collection-based art, library, and garden institution

admission.pricing_note: Adult weekday admission is $29; Friday–Sunday, holidays, and peak periods are $34.

collection.summary: Tens of thousands of works with strengths in British and European art, complemented by American and Asian collections.

building.scope_note: Area applies to the historic Huntington Art Gallery, not all art spaces across the estate.
building.history: Built as the Huntingtons' Beaux-Arts residence in 1911, the house opened as an art gallery in 1928 and was restored in 2008.

financial_value.basis: Organization-wide endowment and beneficial interests covering the library, art museum, and gardens.

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission is $30.

collection.summary: More than 500,000 works from ancient cultures through contemporary art, with major Asian, American, European, and ancient holdings.

notable_works.title: The Daughters of Edward Darley Boit

building.scope_note: Area covers the full complex after the 2010 expansion.
building.history: The museum moved to Guy Lowell's Huntington Avenue building in 1909; later additions include work by I. M. Pei and Foster + Partners.

financial_value.basis: Audited endowment net assets including managed endowment, pledges, and perpetual trusts.

title: Smithsonian American Art Museum
value: 937,716 visitors in 2025 (Art Newspaper shared global rank 80)

admission.pricing_note: Admission is always free and no tickets are required.

collection.summary: Approximately 43,000 works by more than 7,000 artists in the official 2017 fact sheet, surveying art made in the United States.

notable_works.title: Electronic Superhighway: Continental U.S., Alaska, Hawaii

building.scope_note: The Old Patent Office Building is shared with the National Portrait Gallery.
building.history: Constructed in phases from 1836 to 1868, the historic Patent Office reopened for SAAM and the National Portrait Gallery in 1968.

title: National Portrait Gallery
value: 937,716 visitors in 2025 (Art Newspaper shared global rank 80)

admission.pricing_note: General admission is free.

collection.summary: Approximately 26,215 portraits and related objects in the FY2022 fact sheet, including the only complete U.S. presidential portrait collection outside the White House.

building.scope_note: The Old Patent Office Building is shared with the Smithsonian American Art Museum.
building.history: The National Portrait Gallery opened in the restored Old Patent Office Building in 1968.

title: Whitney Museum of American Art
institution_type: American modern and contemporary art museum

admission.pricing_note: Adults $30; visitors 25 and under are free, with additional free periods.

collection.summary: More than 27,000 works by over 4,100 American artists, focused on 20th- and 21st-century art.

building.scope_note: Area is the current Meatpacking District building.
building.history: The Renzo Piano-designed building opened in 2015 with indoor and outdoor exhibition spaces beside the High Line.

financial_value.basis: Board-designated and donor-restricted endowment funds at June 30, 2025.

nearby_hotel.name: Country Inn & Suites
nearby_hotel.address: 3251 Deposit Drive NE, Grand Rapids, MI 49546

admission.pricing_note: Standard adult daytime admission was $25 at verification; special events may be priced separately.

collection.summary: A sculpture collection of nearly 300 works integrated with indoor galleries and a 158-acre botanical campus.

building.scope_note: The institution reports a 158-acre campus; a reliable total indoor square-foot figure was not located.
building.history: Frederik Meijer Gardens & Sculpture Park opened in 1995 and combines horticulture with a permanent sculpture program.

financial_value.basis: Organization-wide net assets reported in the 2025 annual report.

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission is $20; special exhibitions may carry an additional charge.

collection.summary: American art, international textiles and costume, and art from Africa, Oceania, and the Indigenous Americas.

building.scope_note: Area is for the current Golden Gate Park museum building.
building.history: The current copper-clad museum opened in 2005, replacing the earlier de Young building on the same site.

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission is $24; admission is free on Thursdays and some exhibitions are separately ticketed.

collection.summary: Approximately 80,000 works spanning about 6,000 years and cultures from six continents.

building.scope_note: Year and area describe the Nancy and Rich Kinder Building, one component of the multi-building MFAH campus.
building.history: The Kinder Building opened in 2020 as the center of the museum's modern and contemporary collection.

financial_value.basis: Museum-wide net assets, including Bayou Bend, Rienzi, and the Glassell School, at June 30, 2025.

title: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art

admission.pricing_note: General admission is free; some temporary exhibitions and events require paid tickets.

collection.summary: More than 4,100 works surveying five centuries of American art.

building.scope_note: The original complex opened in 2011; a 114,000-square-foot expansion opened in June 2026, but no reliable whole-campus area was located.
building.history: Moshe Safdie designed the museum's bridge-like pavilions around two spring-fed ponds in the Ozarks.

title: Los Angeles County Museum of Art

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission for non-Los Angeles County residents is $30; county residents pay $25.

collection.summary: More than 155,000 works spanning art history and cultures from antiquity to the present.

building.scope_note: The David Geffen Galleries opened in 2026 with 110,000 square feet of exhibition space; that partial figure is not used as whole-campus area.
building.history: LACMA became an independent museum in 1961; its Peter Zumthor-designed David Geffen Galleries opened April 19, 2026.

financial_value.basis: Museum net assets at June 30, 2025; collection objects are not capitalized in the statements.

admission.pricing_note: General admission to the permanent collection is free; some special exhibitions require tickets.

collection.summary: Approximately 66,500 works spanning 6,000 years of art from around the world.

building.scope_note: Area is the completed museum complex after the Rafael Viñoly expansion and renovation.
building.history: The original neoclassical building opened in 1916; later Marcel Breuer and Rafael Viñoly additions culminated in the completed campus in 2013.

financial_value.basis: Net assets reported in the museum's FY2025 IRS filing.

admission.pricing_note: Standard adult admission is $30; discounted and pay-what-you-wish periods are available.

collection.summary: Approximately 8,000 modern and contemporary works held across the Solomon R. Guggenheim Foundation's collection.

building.scope_note: No reliable current whole-building area was located in the cited official architecture material.
building.history: Frank Lloyd Wright's spiraling museum building opened in 1959 and is part of a UNESCO World Heritage serial property.

nearby_hotel.name: Omni Los Angeles Hotel
nearby_hotel.address: 251 South Olive Street, Los Angeles, CA 90012

admission.pricing_note: General admission to the permanent collection is free; some exhibitions and events are ticketed.

collection.summary: More than 2,000 postwar and contemporary works by nearly 200 artists.

notable_works.title: Infinity Mirrored Room—The Souls of Millions of Light Years Away

building.scope_note: Area is for the downtown Los Angeles museum building.
building.history: The veil-and-vault building opened in 2015 across from Walt Disney Concert Hall.

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission is $30 and is valid for two consecutive days at museum locations.

collection.summary: More than 240,000 objects spanning artistic achievement across cultures and centuries.

building.scope_note: No single reliable current whole-building area was located in the cited official sources.
building.history: The landmark main building opened in 1928; a Frank Gehry-led interior renovation and expansion opened new public spaces in 2021.

financial_value.basis: Museum net assets reported for fiscal 2025.

admission.pricing_note: General admission to the permanent collection is free; special exhibitions may require tickets.

collection.summary: More than 50,000 works spanning world art, including the largest public collection of Fabergé outside Russia.

notable_works.title: Imperial Easter Egg with Revolving Miniatures

building.scope_note: The 2010 McGlothlin Wing added 165,000 square feet; no reliable current whole-campus total was located.
building.history: VMFA opened in 1936 as a state-supported art museum and expanded substantially with the 2010 Rick Mather-designed wing.

financial_value.basis: Net assets of the Virginia Museum of Fine Arts Foundation, not the Commonwealth-operated museum itself.

title: San Francisco Museum of Modern Art

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission is $30; some exhibitions and programs may be separately ticketed.

collection.summary: Modern and contemporary painting, sculpture, photography, architecture and design, and media arts.

building.scope_note: Approximate total after the 2016 Snøhetta expansion, derived from official descriptions of the existing and added areas.
building.history: Mario Botta's downtown building opened in 1995; a major Snøhetta expansion opened in 2016.

admission.pricing_note: General admission is free; special exhibitions may require tickets.

collection.summary: More than 100,000 objects covering 5,000 years and cultures from six continents.

building.scope_note: The 1915 main building has 1974 and 2006 additions; no reliable current whole-building area was located.
building.history: The McKim, Mead & White building opened in 1915 and was enlarged by Kenzo Tange in 1974 and Michael Graves in 2006.

title: The Frick Collection
institution_type: Old Master and European decorative arts museum

admission.pricing_note: Adult admission is $30; pay-what-you-wish hours are offered on Wednesdays.

collection.summary: European paintings, sculpture, and decorative arts from the Renaissance through the late 19th century, displayed in Henry Clay Frick's former residence.

building.scope_note: Area is for the renovated Frick campus after the 2021–2025 project.
building.history: Henry Clay Frick's 1914 mansion became a museum in 1935; a major Selldorf-led renovation reopened in 2025.

title: Legion of Honor
value: 406,265 visitors in 2025 (separately reported campus of Art Newspaper global rank 92)

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission is $20; special exhibitions may carry an additional charge.

collection.summary: European paintings, sculpture, decorative arts, ancient art, and works on paper within the Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco.

building.scope_note: No reliable current whole-building area was located.
building.history: The Beaux-Arts museum opened in 1924 as a memorial to California soldiers and was modeled on Paris's Palais de la Légion d'Honneur.

sources.note: Attendance is reported separately beneath the Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco survey row.

title: The Met Cloisters
value: 281,807 visitors in 2025 (separately reported campus of Art Newspaper global rank 5)

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission is $30 and includes the Met's locations; New York State residents and eligible local students may pay what they wish.

collection.summary: Approximately 2,000 works of medieval European art, with particular strengths from the 12th through the 15th centuries.

notable_works.title: The Unicorn Rests in a Garden

building.scope_note: No reliable current whole-building area was located.
building.history: The branch opened in 1938 in a purpose-built complex incorporating architectural elements from medieval European cloisters.

sources.note: Attendance is reported separately beneath The Metropolitan Museum of Art survey row.

title: Getty Villa Museum
value: 188,952 visitors in 2025 (separately reported campus of Art Newspaper global rank 58)
institution_type: Ancient Greek, Roman, and Etruscan art museum

nearby_hotel.name: Brentwood Inn
nearby_hotel.address: 12200 West Sunset Boulevard, Los Angeles, CA 90049

admission.pricing_note: Admission is free, but a timed-entry reservation is required; parking fees apply.

collection.summary: Ancient Greek, Roman, and Etruscan art displayed in a re-created Roman country house setting.

building.scope_note: The original Villa opened in 1974 and reopened after renovation in 2006; no reliable whole-building area was located.
building.history: Modeled after the Villa dei Papiri at Herculaneum, the museum opened in 1974 and was renovated and re-landscaped for its 2006 reopening.

sources.note: Attendance is reported separately beneath the J. Paul Getty Museum survey row.

title: Renwick Gallery
value: 177,073 visitors in 2025 (separately reported campus of Art Newspaper global rank 80)
institution_type: American craft and decorative arts museum

admission.pricing_note: Admission is free; timed passes may be used for unusually popular exhibitions.

collection.summary: Nearly 2,000 works of contemporary craft and decorative art in wood, fiber, metal, glass, and ceramic media.

building.scope_note: No reliable current whole-building area was located.
building.history: Completed in 1874 as Washington's first purpose-built art museum, the building became the Smithsonian American Art Museum's craft branch in 1972 and was renovated from 2013 to 2015.

sources.note: Attendance is reported separately beneath the Smithsonian American Art Museum survey row.

ranking.source_name: The Washington Post 20 Best Art Museums in America

admission.pricing_note: Adult general admission is $20 for nonresidents; residents of Wayne, Oakland, and Macomb counties receive free general admission.

collection.summary: More than 65,000 works spanning cultures and eras, with strengths in American, European, African, Asian, and Indigenous art.

building.scope_note: Area is for the museum complex after its wings and 2007 renovation.
building.history: The Beaux-Arts building opened in 1927; later Gunnar Birkerts wings and a 2007 renovation expanded and reorganized the museum.

ranking.source_name: The Washington Post 20 Best Art Museums in America

admission.pricing_note: General admission is free; some special exhibitions require tickets.

collection.summary: More than 38,000 objects spanning 5,000 years and cultures from six continents.

building.scope_note: The 1904 main building received a major East Building addition in 2013; no reliable current whole-building area was located.
building.history: The Cass Gilbert building was created for the 1904 World's Fair and became the museum's permanent home; David Chipperfield's East Building opened in 2013.

ranking.source_name: The Washington Post 20 Best Art Museums in America

admission.pricing_note: General admission is free; advance reservations may be requested for some visits or programs.

collection.summary: More than 95,000 objects, including the world's largest public collection of works by Henri Matisse.

building.scope_note: Area is for the museum building described in the institution's official fact material.
building.history: Founded in 1914, the museum moved into its John Russell Pope-designed neoclassical building in 1929.
